name: fix
context: Auto-fix ESLint violations with --fix, --fix-dry-run, and programmatic API — batch fix, preview, and selective fixing
info: This skill covers ESLint's auto-fix capabilities: running --fix to correct fixable violations in-place, using --fix-dry-run to preview changes without writing, filtering fix types with --fix-type, and using the ESLint Node.js API for programmatic fixing in custom tooling. Agents use this skill when asked to fix lint errors, clean up code, or integrate auto-fixing into build pipelines and pre-commit hooks. Not all rules are fixable — unfixable violations require manual code changes. See [[eslint/rules]] for which rules support auto-fix and [[eslint/troubleshooting]] for when --fix produces unexpected results.
body:
# ESLint Auto-Fix

ESLint's `--fix` flag automatically corrects fixable rule violations. Approximately 90 of ESLint's built-in rules are fixable. Plugin rules may also support auto-fix.

## Basic Usage

```bash
# Fix all fixable issues in current directory
npx eslint --fix .

# Fix specific files
npx eslint --fix src/index.js src/utils.js

# Fix all files matching a pattern
npx eslint --fix "src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}"

# Fix only specific directories
npx eslint --fix src/ lib/
```

## Preview Fixes (Dry Run)

```bash
# Show what would be fixed without writing files
npx eslint --fix-dry-run .

# Output proposed fixes as JSON for review
npx eslint --fix-dry-run --format json . > fixes.json
```

## Fix Types

Filter which categories of fixes to apply:

```bash
# Only fix formatting/layout issues (quotes, semi, indent)
npx eslint --fix --fix-type layout .

# Only fix problems (no-unused-vars, no-undef)
npx eslint --fix --fix-type problem .

# Only fix suggestions
npx eslint --fix --fix-type suggestion .

# Only fix inline directives
npx eslint --fix --fix-type directive .

# Combine types
npx eslint --fix --fix-type problem --fix-type suggestion .
```

| Fix Type | Examples |
|----------|----------|
| `problem` | Fixes for correctness issues |
| `suggestion` | Preference-based improvements |
| `layout` | Whitespace, semicolons, quotes |
| `directive` | Inline comment fixes |

## Common Fixable Rules

| Rule | What --fix Does |
|------|------------------|
| `semi` | Adds or removes semicolons |
| `quotes` | Changes quote style |
| `eqeqeq` | Replaces `==` with `===` |
| `prefer-const` | Changes `let` to `const` |
| `no-var` | Changes `var` to `let`/`const` |
| `no-extra-semi` | Removes extra semicolons |
| `arrow-body-style` | Converts arrow function bodies |
| `object-shorthand` | Uses property shorthand |
| `prefer-template` | Converts concatenation to template literals |
| `curly` | Adds braces to if/else/for |

## CI Integration

```bash
# In CI: fail if there are any fixable issues (don't auto-fix)
npx eslint --max-warnings 0 .

# In CI: check if fix would change anything
npx eslint --fix-dry-run --format json . | jq '[.[] | select(.output != null)] | length'
```

## Pre-Commit Hook (with lint-staged)

```json
// package.json
{
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}": ["eslint --fix"]
  }
}
```

Setup:

```bash
npm install --save-dev lint-staged husky
npx husky init
echo 'npx lint-staged' > .husky/pre-commit
```

## Programmatic API

```javascript
import { ESLint } from "eslint";

const eslint = new ESLint({ fix: true });
const results = await eslint.lintFiles(["src/**/*.js"]);

// Write fixes to disk
await ESLint.outputFixes(results);

// Get remaining (unfixable) errors
const formatter = await eslint.loadFormatter("stylish");
const output = await formatter.format(results);
if (output) console.log(output);
```

## Caching with Fix

```bash
# Cache + fix: only process changed files
npx eslint --fix --cache .

# Clear cache when config changes
rm .eslintcache && npx eslint --fix --cache .
```

## Unfixable Violations

Some common rules are NOT auto-fixable and require manual code changes:

| Rule | Why Not Fixable |
|------|------------------|
| `no-unused-vars` | ESLint can't know if removal is safe |
| `no-console` | Removing console calls changes behavior |
| `no-debugger` | Removing debugger changes behavior |
| `no-undef` | ESLint can't determine the correct fix |
| `no-unreachable` | Removing code changes behavior |

For these, the agent must understand the code context and apply the appropriate fix manually.

sections.title: Basic fix usage
sections.body: Run `npx eslint --fix .` to auto-fix all fixable violations in the current directory. ESLint modifies files in-place. Only fixable rules are corrected — unfixable violations are still reported. Always review changes after fixing, especially in unfamiliar codebases.

sections.title: Preview with dry run
sections.body: Use `--fix-dry-run` to see what would change without writing files. Combine with `--format json` to get machine-readable output. The `output` field in JSON results contains the fixed source code — compare with the original `source` field to see changes.

sections.title: Selective fix types
sections.body: Use `--fix-type` to apply only specific categories: `problem` for correctness, `suggestion` for preferences, `layout` for formatting, `directive` for inline comments. Multiple types can be combined. This is useful when you want to fix formatting without changing code logic.

sections.title: Pre-commit integration
sections.body: Use `lint-staged` with `husky` to run `eslint --fix` on staged files before commit. This ensures only clean code is committed. Install with `npm install --save-dev lint-staged husky`, then configure `lint-staged` in package.json to run `eslint --fix` on matching files.

sections.title: Programmatic API
sections.body: Use the `ESLint` class from the `eslint` package for programmatic fixing. Create an instance with `fix: true`, call `lintFiles()`, then `ESLint.outputFixes(results)` to write fixes. The `results` array contains per-file data with `errorCount`, `warningCount`, `fixableErrorCount`, and `output` (fixed source).

links.context: Plugin rules may also be fixable

related.note: Rules must be configured before fix can correct them
A config file must exist for fix to work

scripts.name: Fix ESLint Issues
scripts.description: Run ESLint --fix on project files with caching, reporting unfixable issues

target.description: File, directory, or glob to fix

depends.reason: An ESLint config file must exist for --fix to work

author: dojo-community
